title: Higher-Order Operations, Map, Filter, and Fold · Mastery Check
summary: Summarize a stream of service records without hand-written accumulator loops. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a tested map-filter-fold analytics pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use mapping for one-output-per-input transformations without smuggling filtering effects.
Design total predicates that retain records for explicit, testable reasons.
Define an accumulator identity and invariant before implementing a fold.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Map Transform value-and-effect pipeline for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Map Transform assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Three intake records need normalize → validate → score transformations. A rejected record must retain its reason, and adding a fourth transformation must not change earlier function contracts. Competency probe: Use mapping for one-output-per-input transformations without smuggling filtering effects.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Map Transform has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
